instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `ensure-symbol` that signals an error if its argument is not a symbol; otherwise returns the argument. Evaluates the argument once.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 NAME))
            (UNLESS (SYMBOLP #:G1) (ERROR "Expected a symbol, got ~S" #:G1))
            #:G1)
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 NAME))
            (UNLESS (SYMBOLP #:G112)
              (ERROR "Expected a symbol, got ~S" #:G112))
            #:G112)

--- stderr ---
